title: Match filtering supports season but not an explicit date range
evidence: tools.go:11 toolSearchMatches and data.go:448 FilterMatches accept team/competition/season only; no start/end date args are exposed (search_matches inputSchema, main.go:138). R4 is 'date range and/or season' — season satisfies it, but date-range narrowing is unavailable.
suggestion: Add optional from/to date arguments to search_matches and filter on Match.DateTime.

title: get_standings with no competition mixes all competitions into one table
evidence: tools.go:150 toolStandings only requires season; data.go:549 Standings calls FilterMatches('', competition, season) so an empty competition aggregates Brasileirão + Copa do Brasil + Libertadores, producing a points table over knockout cups too.
suggestion: Require/encourage a competition for standings, or default to Brasileirão when omitted.

title: Integer arg coercion treats explicit 0 as 'use default'
evidence: main.go:320 getInt returns the default when the stringified value is "0"; harmless here since season default is 0 and limits should be positive, but it conflates 'unset' and 'zero'.
suggestion: Distinguish missing keys from zero values by checking presence in the args map before coercion.

title: Team matching is substring-based and can over-match short names
evidence: data.go:113 teamMatches and data.go:472 FilterMatchesH2H use strings.Contains; a query like 'Sao Paulo' or a short token could match multiple distinct clubs. No exact-match mode is offered.
suggestion: Offer an exact/normalized-equality option in addition to substring search for disambiguation.

title: Implements MCP protocol with zero third-party dependencies
evidence: go.mod declares no requires and there is no go.sum; main.go:38-120 hand-rolls the JSON-RPC/MCP types rather than using an MCP SDK.
suggestion: None — noted as a positive: minimal dependency surface. A real MCP SDK would reduce protocol-maintenance risk if the spec evolves.
